canonical_question: What are the essential graph algorithms (BFS, DFS, Dijkstra, A*)?
constraints: Dijkstra requires non-negative edge weights; use Bellman-Ford for graphs with negative edges
A* requires an admissible heuristic (h(n) <= actual cost) to guarantee optimal paths
Topological sort is only defined for DAGs; cyclic graphs have no valid ordering
Floyd-Warshall uses O(V^3) time and O(V^2) space — impractical for V > ~5,000
BFS only finds shortest paths in unweighted or unit-weight graphs

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Need to solve network flow or matching problems (max-flow, min-cut, bipartite matching)
Working with probabilistic or Bayesian graph models
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Probabilistic graphical models / Bayesian network inference

inputs_needed.question: What type of graph are you working with?
What problem are you trying to solve?
